tool.system_prompt:
Search for files under a directory. You can filter by filename pattern (glob) and optionally grep file contents using a regular expression.

Notes:
- content_pattern is treated as a Python regular expression.
- If content_pattern is empty, only filename matching is performed.
- For performance and safety, binary-like files can be excluded from content searching.parameterstypeobjectpropertiesroot_pathtypestringdescriptionparam.root_path.descriptionRoot directory to start searching from (default: current directory).name_patterntypestringdescriptionparam.name_pattern.descriptionFilename glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', 'test_*'). If omitted, all files are considered.content_patterntypestringdescriptionparam.content_pattern.descriptionRegular expression to search within files. If provided, only files containing at least one matching line are returned.case_sensitivetypebooleandescriptionparam.case_sensitive.descriptionWhether the content search is case-sensitive (default: false).max_resultstypeintegerdescriptionparam.max_results.descriptionMaximum number of matched files to return (default: 50).exclude_binarytypebooleandescriptionparam.exclude_binary.descriptionWhen content_pattern is set, exclude files that appear to be binary (default: true).defaultbinary_sniff_bytestypeintegerdescriptionparam.binary_sniff_bytes.descriptionNumber of leading bytes used to detect binary-like files (default: 8192).defaultfast_read_threshold_bytestypeintegerdescriptionparam.fast_read_threshold_bytes.descriptionFiles smaller than this threshold are fully read() for faster searching (default: 8000000 ≈ 8MB). Larger files are scanned line-by-line.defaultrequired.git__pycache__.venvvenvnode_modules.idea.vscodecoverage.pyc.pyd.so.dll.exe.bin.zip.tar.gz.7z.png.jpg.jpeg.gif.icoHeuristically determine whether a file is likely binary.\x00
